request.context_summary: Security-focused code review of authentication handlers (login, logout, refresh) in Go Lambda functions

findings.issue: Distinct messaging for unconfirmed users discloses account state, enabling user enumeration and targeted attacks.
findings.suggestion: Return a generic 401 message for all authentication failures, or only disclose confirmation status after a verified step (e.g., resend-confirmation flow).

findings.issue: Token responses lack explicit no-store cache headers, so access/refresh tokens can be cached by browsers or intermediaries.
findings.suggestion: Set Cache-Control: no-store (and Pragma: no-cache) on login responses that include tokens; consider adding these headers in middleware for auth endpoints.

findings.issue: Token refresh responses also omit no-store cache headers, risking token persistence in caches.
findings.suggestion: Add Cache-Control: no-store (and Pragma: no-cache) for refresh responses; centralize in middleware or per-handler headers.

response.summary: Found three security concerns: user enumeration via unconfirmed-user messaging and missing no-store cache headers for token responses in login/refresh.
